channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5 in eastern Pacific, U.S. military says
item.description: The U.S. military said Sunday that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said Sunday that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins the Masters for second year in a row
item.description: Rory McIlroy goes back-to-back at the Masters to join Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods.
item.content:encoded:
Rory McIlroy goes back-to-back at the Masters to join Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods.

(Image credit: Ashley Landis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A stampede at a Haitian mountaintop fortress kills at least 25 people and injures dozens
item.description: A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has killed at least 25 people and injured dozens of others, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has killed at least 25 people and injured dozens of others, authorities said.

(Image credit: Ketlain Difficile)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Peru election results delayed after thousands get a one-day voting extension
item.description: Voters will have to wait until at least Monday to learn the outcome of Sunday's presidential election after the process was mired with logistical issues that left thousands unable to cast ballots.
item.content:encoded:
Voters will have to wait until at least Monday to learn the outcome of Sunday's presidential election after the process was mired with logistical issues that left thousands unable to cast ballots.

(Image credit: Guadalupe Pardo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with American pontiff
item.description: President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.-born global leader of the Catholic Church is "doing a very good job."
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.-born global leader of the Catholic Church is "doing a very good job."

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Fighting assault allegations, Eric Swalwell suspends his bid for California governor
item.description: The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.
item.content:encoded:
The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.

(Image credit: Rich Pedroncelli)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán concedes defeat, ending 16 years in power
item.description: Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.
item.content:encoded:
Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.

(Image credit: Attila Kisbenedek)
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: A country-by-country glance at Pope Leo XIV's trip to Africa
item.description: Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Asha Bhosle, the voice of Bollywood, has died aged 92
item.description: The Bollywood legend was one of the world's most recorded artists — who, by her own reckoning, made more than 12,000 songs.
item.content:encoded:
The Bollywood legend was one of the world's most recorded artists — who, by her own reckoning, made more than 12,000 songs.

(Image credit: Steven Siewert)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Sweet Treat
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KUT listener Nell Newton and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KUT listener Nell Newton and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz
